decision-tree.root-question: Does the operation write to the database (save/update/delete/bulk_*) or trigger a side effect (email, Celery, webhook)?

branch.conclusion: Read operation: use [[django-selectors]]. HTTP-only glue: keep in the view. Stop here.

branch.question: Are multiple models written OR side effects triggered as part of the same business action?
Does the model define `clean()` or composite constraints?

branch.conclusion: Inline `Model.objects.create(...)` is acceptable. Promote to service only when a second caller appears.
Extract into a service so `full_clean()` is called before `save()`. Even a single-model write needs the validation contract.
Extract into a service. Apply ALL five rules: kwarg-only, atomic boundary, full_clean, domain exceptions, on_commit side-effects.

example.trace: POST /orders/ creates Order + N OrderItems + sends notification → q1=yes (write) → q2=yes (multi-model + side effect) → r2-atomic-boundary (full service)
